examName: AWS Certified AI Practitioner (AIF-C01) Practice Exam

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: A data scientist is explaining machine learning concepts to business stakeholders. Which of the following best describes the relationship between AI, ML, and deep learning?
options: AI and ML are the same thing; deep learning is a completely different field
AI is the broader field; ML is a subset of AI; deep learning is a subset of ML
Deep learning is the broadest field; ML and AI are subsets of deep learning
They are three independent fields with no relationship to each other
questions.explanation: AI is the broadest field encompassing any technique that enables machines to mimic human intelligence. ML is a subset of AI focused on learning from data. Deep learning is a subset of ML using neural networks with multiple layers.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: An organization wants to predict customer churn based on historical purchase patterns, demographics, and user behavior. Which supervised learning approach is most appropriate?
questions.explanation: Predicting customer churn involves classifying customers into two categories (will churn or will not churn) based on features. This is a binary classification problem, which is a supervised learning task.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: Which of the following are valid types of data used in AI/ML models? (Select THREE)
questions.explanation: Valid data types include labeled and unlabeled data, plus multiple formats like tabular, time-series, image, and text data. Intangible data is not a standard ML data type category.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: What does 'training and inferencing' mean in the context of machine learning?
options: Training is building the model; inferencing is the process of deriving predictions using the trained model
Training and inferencing are the same process performed on different datasets
Training is a one-time activity; inferencing is repeated thousands of times
Training requires labeled data; inferencing only works with unlabeled data
questions.explanation: Training is the process of teaching the model using data. Inferencing is applying that trained model to make predictions on new, unseen data.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: A retail company wants to identify groups of similar customers without predefined labels. Which learning approach should they use?
questions.explanation: Clustering is an unsupervised learning technique that groups similar data points without predefined labels. This is ideal for customer segmentation.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: Match each ML development lifecycle component to its description.
rightItems: Adjusting model parameters to optimize performance
Cleaning and formatting raw data for model consumption
Testing model performance on validation/test datasets
Putting the trained model into production
Teaching the model to find patterns in data
questions.explanation: Data pre-processing prepares data; model training teaches patterns; evaluation assesses performance; hyperparameter tuning optimizes; deployment puts it in production.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: Arrange the ML development lifecycle steps in the correct order.
questions.explanation: The correct ML pipeline sequence starts with data collection, followed by analysis, preprocessing, feature engineering, training, evaluation, deployment, and ongoing monitoring.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: What is the primary purpose of exploratory data analysis (EDA) in the ML development lifecycle?
options: To train the first version of the model
To understand data patterns, distributions, and anomalies
To deploy the model to production
To scale the model for inference
questions.explanation: EDA helps data scientists understand the data through visualization and statistical analysis, identifying patterns, distributions, outliers, and missing values before model training.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: Which AWS managed AI services are mentioned in the exam scope? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Amazon SageMaker AI and Amazon Transcribe are both in-scope services. AWS Brainstorm and Amazon DataMind are not real AWS services.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: A company is deciding between using a pre-trained open-source model and building a custom model from scratch. Which factor should NOT influence this decision?
options: Time to market and development costs
The color scheme of the company website
questions.explanation: The color scheme of a website has no relevance to ML model selection. Relevant factors include cost, time, domain-specific needs, data availability, and business objectives.

questions.text: Which of the following best describes what a token is in the context of generative AI?
options: A physical device used for authentication in AI systems
A small unit of text (word, subword, or character) that LLMs process
A cryptocurrency used to pay for cloud services
A security certificate for AI applications
questions.explanation: In GenAI, tokens are fundamental units of text that language models process. They can be words, subwords, or characters depending on the tokenization scheme.

questions.text: What is an embedding in the context of generative AI?
options: A method of hiding data inside other data
A numerical representation of text or images that captures semantic meaning
A type of neural network layer
questions.explanation: Embeddings are vector representations (arrays of numbers) that capture the semantic meaning of text, images, or other content, enabling models to understand relationships between concepts.

questions.text: Which of the following are core advantages of generative AI solutions? (Select THREE)
questions.explanation: GenAI advantages include adaptability, responsiveness, and simplicity. However, GenAI is not perfectly accurate and still requires human oversight in many scenarios.

questions.text: A company is considering GenAI for customer service but is concerned about hallucinations. What does hallucination mean in GenAI?
options: When the model takes too long to generate responses
When the model generates plausible but false or irrelevant information
When the model refuses to answer a question
When the model provides responses in the wrong language
questions.explanation: Hallucinations occur when GenAI models generate content that sounds plausible but is actually false, misleading, or not grounded in reality. This is a key limitation to consider.

questions.text: What is the primary purpose of chunking in the context of generative AI applications?
options: Breaking down large documents into smaller, manageable pieces for processing
Dividing the cost of API calls among multiple departments
Splitting the model across multiple servers
questions.explanation: Chunking breaks large documents or texts into smaller segments that can be processed more effectively by models, especially in RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) scenarios.

questions.text: Which AWS services and features are designed to help develop GenAI applications? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Amazon Bedrock PartyRock and Amazon Q are GenAI-specific development tools. AWS Lambda Edge, Amazon SageMaker JumpStart, and Amazon RDS serve different purposes.

questions.text: What are transformer-based LLMs and what is their significance in GenAI?
options: Older models that have been replaced by modern architectures
Models that convert text to images
Deep learning models using attention mechanisms that form the foundation of modern language models
Models that require manual parameter tuning
questions.explanation: Transformer-based LLMs use attention mechanisms to process sequences of data efficiently and form the foundation of most modern GenAI models like GPT and Claude.

questions.text: Match each GenAI limitation to a potential business impact.
rightItems: Difficulty explaining why model made specific decision
Generation of false or misleading information
Slow response times affecting user experience
questions.explanation: Hallucinations cause false info; non-determinism causes variable outputs; interpretability makes explanation hard; high latency causes slow responses.

questions.text: Which factors should be considered when selecting a GenAI model for a business application? (Select THREE)
questions.explanation: Relevant selection criteria include performance needs, compliance requirements, capabilities, cost, and constraints. CEO hardware preferences are not a technical consideration.

questions.text: What is the foundation model lifecycle and which phase comes after model pre-training?
options: Fine-tuning and evaluation come after pre-training
questions.explanation: The foundation model lifecycle includes data selection, model selection, pre-training, fine-tuning, evaluation, deployment, and feedback. Fine-tuning and evaluation follow pre-training.

questions.text: A company wants to build a customer Q&A system using internal documents without retraining the model. Which approach should they implement?
options: Fine-tuning the model with all documents
Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) using Amazon Bedrock Knowledge Bases
Creating a separate model for each document
questions.explanation: RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) is the ideal approach. It retrieves relevant documents and provides them as context to the model without expensive retraining or fine-tuning.

questions.text: Which of the following are valid considerations when selecting a pre-trained foundation model? (Select TWO)
options: The month the model was released
questions.explanation: Cost and latency are critical selection criteria. Input/output length is also important. Release month and founder details are not relevant technical considerations.

questions.text: What is prompt engineering and why is it important in foundation model applications?
options: A method for retraining models on new data
The practice of designing effective inputs (prompts) to elicit desired outputs from models
A way to reduce model costs
A technique for converting models to different formats
questions.explanation: Prompt engineering involves crafting effective prompts/instructions to guide models toward desired outputs. It's crucial for improving response quality without retraining.

questions.text: Which of the following are effective prompt engineering techniques? (Select THREE)
questions.explanation: Chain-of-thought, zero-shot, and few-shot are all valid techniques. Ignoring context and being vague contradict best practices for prompt engineering.

questions.text: What does temperature control in foundation models do?
options: Adjusts the physical temperature of the server
Controls the randomness/creativity of model outputs
Determines how fast the model processes requests
Sets the maximum number of tokens allowed
questions.explanation: Temperature is an inference parameter that controls output randomness. Higher temperature = more creative/random; lower temperature = more deterministic/focused.

questions.text: What are AWS services that support storing embeddings in vector databases? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: Amazon OpenSearch Service and Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL support vector storage. Redshift, S3, and DynamoDB don't have native vector database capabilities.

questions.text: What are agents in the context of foundation model applications?
options: Human employees who monitor AI systems
Autonomous entities that use models to break down and execute multi-step tasks
questions.explanation: Agents are autonomous systems that use foundation models to reason through and execute multi-step tasks, making decisions and calling appropriate tools as needed.

questions.text: Arrange the foundation model customization approaches from least to most expensive in terms of compute resources.
questions.explanation: In-context learning is cheapest (no training), followed by RAG, then fine-tuning, then full pre-training which is most resource-intensive.

questions.text: What does fine-tuning a foundation model involve?
options: Adjusting only the input/output layer weights
Training the entire model from scratch
Updating model weights on domain-specific data to adapt the model
Adding more parameters to the model
questions.explanation: Fine-tuning adapts a pre-trained model by training it on domain-specific data, updating weights to specialize the model for a particular task or domain.

questions.text: What is instruction tuning when applied to foundation models?
options: Writing instructions for users of the model
Fine-tuning models to follow specific instructions and command patterns
Tuning the model's instructions for hardware deployment
questions.explanation: Instruction tuning fine-tunes models on examples of instructions and their desired outcomes, making models better at following specific commands and instructions.

questions.text: How should data be prepared for fine-tuning a foundation model? (Select THREE)
options: Data curation to select high-quality examples
Ensuring data is representative of the target domain
questions.explanation: Proper fine-tuning data requires curation, representativeness, and governance. Labeling is important for some types but not all. Unlabeled data alone is insufficient.

questions.text: Which metrics are used to evaluate foundation model performance for text generation tasks? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: ROUGE and BERTScore are appropriate for text generation. BLEU is for machine translation. Pixel Accuracy and Silhouette Coefficient measure different types of tasks.

questions.text: What is Amazon Bedrock Model Evaluation and what does it help determine?
options: The cost of using different models
Whether a foundation model meets business objectives and performs well for specific use cases
The physical hardware requirements for models
questions.explanation: Amazon Bedrock Model Evaluation helps assess whether a model effectively meets business objectives and performs well for specific use cases through structured evaluation.

questions.text: Match each prompt engineering technique to its description.
rightItems: Using reusable prompt structures for consistent outputs
Providing examples in the prompt to guide model behavior
Asking model to explain step-by-step reasoning
Giving no examples, relying on model's base knowledge
questions.explanation: Chain-of-thought = step-by-step; zero-shot = no examples; few-shot = with examples; templates = reusable structures.

questions.text: What are potential security risks in prompt engineering?
options: Prompt injection, poisoning, hijacking, and jailbreaking attacks
questions.explanation: Prompt engineering risks include injection attacks, poisoning (malicious training data), hijacking, and jailbreaking attempts to bypass model safeguards.

questions.text: What is bias in the context of AI systems?
options: A preference for one particular model architecture
Systematic prejudice or unfair treatment in model predictions affecting certain groups
questions.explanation: Bias in AI refers to systematic unfairness or prejudice in model outputs that disadvantages certain demographic groups or populations unfairly.

questions.text: Which of the following are features of responsible AI? (Select THREE)
questions.explanation: Responsible AI focuses on bias, fairness, inclusivity, robustness, safety, and veracity. Maximum profitability is a business goal, not an AI responsibility feature.

questions.text: What is Amazon Bedrock Guardrails and what is its purpose?
options: A tool to measure model accuracy
A service to identify features of responsible AI and apply safety controls
questions.explanation: Amazon Bedrock Guardrails helps identify responsible AI features and apply safeguards and controls to ensure safe, responsible AI deployments.

questions.text: What legal risks should organizations consider when deploying GenAI systems?
options: Intellectual property infringement claims, biased outputs, hallucinations, and loss of customer trust
questions.explanation: Key legal risks include IP infringement claims, biased model outputs causing discrimination, hallucinations leading to misinformation, and damage to customer trust.

questions.text: Which characteristics should training datasets have for responsible AI? (Select THREE)
questions.explanation: Responsible datasets should be inclusive, diverse, curated, balanced, and well-documented. Secrecy contradicts transparency best practices.

questions.text: What is the difference between transparency and explainability in AI models?
options: They mean exactly the same thing
Transparency means the model is clear about data sources; explainability means we can understand the model's decisions
Explainability is for regulations; transparency is for performance
They apply only to image models
questions.explanation: Transparency involves openness about model data and processes; explainability means understanding why the model made specific decisions. Both are important for responsible AI.

questions.text: Which AWS tools help detect and monitor bias in AI systems? (Select TWO)
questions.explanation: SageMaker Clarify and SageMaker Model Monitor are designed tools for detecting and monitoring bias. S3, PartyRock, and Lambda serve different purposes.

questions.text: What is Amazon Augmented AI (A2I) and why is it relevant to responsible AI?
options: A tool to automate all human decision-making
A service that enables human review loops to audit and improve model predictions
questions.explanation: Amazon A2I enables human-in-the-loop workflows where humans review and approve model predictions, which is crucial for responsible and trustworthy AI deployment.

questions.text: How do overfitting and underfitting affect model bias and fairness?
options: They have no relationship to bias
Overfitting can amplify biases in training data; underfitting may miss important patterns affecting fairness
questions.explanation: Overfitting can overemphasize biases present in training data. Underfitting may miss patterns, potentially causing systematic unfairness to certain groups.

questions.text: What environmental considerations should be included in responsible model selection?
options: Energy consumption and carbon footprint of model training and inference
questions.explanation: Responsible AI includes environmental sustainability considerations, including the energy consumption and carbon footprint of model training and deployment.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Which AWS services and features are used to secure AI systems? (Select THREE)
options: Data encryption (at rest and in transit)
questions.explanation: IAM, encryption, and Macie are security tools. PrivateLink is networking. EC2 is compute, not specifically for AI security.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: What is source citation and data lineage in AI systems and why are they important?
options: Documenting where data originated and how it was processed, essential for transparency and compliance
questions.explanation: Source citation and data lineage provide transparency about data origins and transformations, supporting responsible AI, compliance, and traceability.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: What are best practices for secure data engineering in AI projects?
options: Ignoring data quality issues to save time
Assessing data quality, implementing privacy-enhancing technologies, and enforcing access controls
Storing all data in public buckets
questions.explanation: Secure data engineering requires quality assessment, privacy technologies, access control, and data integrity measures to protect sensitive information.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Which of the following are security and privacy considerations for AI systems? (Select THREE)
options: Encryption at rest and in transit
questions.explanation: Security considerations include app security, threat detection, vulnerability management, prompt injection risks, and encryption. CEO preferences are irrelevant.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: What AWS services help with governance and compliance in AI systems? (Select TWO)
options: Amazon S3 (as a data store only)
questions.explanation: AWS Config and Amazon Inspector are governance/compliance tools. CloudTrail is logging. Polly is text-to-speech. S3 is storage.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: What do SageMaker Model Cards provide?
options: Documentation of model characteristics, performance, limitations, and intended use
questions.explanation: SageMaker Model Cards document model characteristics, training data, performance metrics, limitations, and intended use cases for transparency.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: What is a data governance strategy for AI systems and what does it include?
options: Only storing data in one location
Management of data lifecycles, logging, residency, retention, and monitoring
questions.explanation: Data governance includes managing data lifecycles, establishing logging/audit trails, ensuring compliance with data residency requirements, and monitoring data usage.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Which governance processes should be followed for AI solutions?
options: None; move quickly to avoid delays
Establishing policies, review cadence, review strategies, governance frameworks, and team training
questions.explanation: Proper governance includes establishing policies, regular review cadence, governance frameworks (like the Generative AI Security Scoping Matrix), and team training.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: What is the role of feature engineering in the ML development lifecycle?
options: Selecting and transforming raw features to improve model performance
questions.explanation: Feature engineering involves selecting, creating, and transforming relevant features from raw data to improve model performance and predictive power.

questions.domain: Domain 1: Fundamentals of AI and ML
questions.text: Describe the different types of ML model inference.
options: Batch inference processes multiple records at once; real-time inference processes individual requests immediately
Inference and training are the same
questions.explanation: Batch inference processes multiple records efficiently (good for large datasets); real-time inference handles individual requests immediately (for on-demand predictions).

questions.text: What are multimodal models in the context of generative AI?
options: Models that can process and generate multiple types of data (text, images, audio, video)
Models that are trained multiple times
questions.explanation: Multimodal models process and generate multiple data types (text, images, audio, video), enabling more versatile AI applications.

questions.text: What are diffusion models and what are they used for?
options: Models for spreading information across networks
Deep learning models that generate images and other content by gradually denoising
questions.explanation: Diffusion models are generative models that create images and content by starting with noise and gradually refining it, used in image generation applications.

questions.text: What is the relationship between RAG and knowledge bases in foundation model applications?
options: Knowledge bases store documents that RAG retrieves and provides as context to foundation models
RAG is only for text classification
questions.explanation: RAG retrieves relevant documents from knowledge bases and provides them as context to foundation models, enabling domain-specific Q&A without retraining.

questions.text: What is the impact of input/output length constraints on foundation model selection?
options: Different models have different maximum token limits; this affects their suitability for tasks with long documents or required outputs
questions.explanation: Different foundation models have different input/output token limits. This is critical when selecting models for tasks requiring long context or detailed responses.

questions.text: What is prompt caching and what advantage does it provide?
options: Reusing previously processed prompt segments to reduce latency and costs
A type of prompt injection attack
questions.explanation: Prompt caching stores and reuses previously processed prompt segments, reducing processing latency and API costs for repeated queries.

questions.text: What does veracity mean in the context of responsible AI?
options: The truthfulness and accuracy of model outputs
The cost of using the model
questions.explanation: Veracity refers to ensuring model outputs are truthful, accurate, and reliable, which is essential for building trust in AI systems.

questions.text: What is human-centered design in the context of explainable AI?
options: Designing AI explanations that users can understand and trust, centering on human usability
Automating all decisions without human input
questions.explanation: Human-centered explainable AI design focuses on creating explanations that are understandable to users, supporting informed decision-making and trust.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: What is the Generative AI Security Scoping Matrix and its purpose?
options: A tool to increase model accuracy
A governance framework for systematically identifying and managing security risks in GenAI systems
questions.explanation: The Generative AI Security Scoping Matrix is a governance framework that helps organizations systematically scope and manage security considerations in GenAI deployments.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: Which compliance and governance tools does AWS provide? (Select THREE)
questions.explanation: AWS Audit Manager, AWS Artifact, and AWS CloudTrail are compliance/governance tools. Polly is text-to-speech; Glue is for data integration.

questions.domain: Domain 5: Security, Compliance, and Governance for AI Solutions
questions.text: What is the AWS shared responsibility model and how does it apply to AI systems?
options: AWS is responsible for all security
Customers are responsible for all security
AWS secures infrastructure; customers secure their data, access control, and application configuration
Security is not a shared responsibility
questions.explanation: The AWS shared responsibility model divides security: AWS manages infrastructure/service security; customers manage data, access control, encryption keys, and application security.
